x.tweet: What happens when a self-help figure claims to be god through quantum mysticism and unverifiable inner states? An evidence-based audit.

vertical_caption: When a spiritual teacher claims quantum physics proves consciousness creates reality — but the physics doesn't say that. We traced the claims.
shorts_description_lead: An evidence-based examination of public claims made by Leo Gura, founder of Actualized.org, from quantum mysticism to the safety profile of the drugs he recommends to followers.

video.title: The Snake Oil Index: Leo Gura
video.script: Leo Gura built a paid empire on a single claim: that he's attained god consciousness through quantum physics and high-dose psychedelics. The problem? The quantum physics doesn't actually say what he claims. Mainstream interpretations require zero role for consciousness in wave collapse. His unfalsifiable claims about inner states can't be verified. His verifiable claims about physics and pharmacology don't hold up. We've traced the sources — from his own forum to peer-reviewed safety literature — and documented where the evidence stops. Check the breakdown.
